subtitle: The 3-kilometer golden-sand city beach of Las Palmas de Gran Canaria, turned into a calm Atlantic lagoon by La Barra — a 100,000-year-old natural reef that breaks the ocean offshore — with surf at one end and a world-class auditorium at the other.
spike_statement: A 3 km golden-sand city beach in the Canary Islands, calmed into a sheltered lagoon by La Barra, a 100,000-year-old natural reef breakwater 200 m offshore.

levers.photo_tone: fine golden sand and a calm turquoise Atlantic lagoon in a bright subtropical city, the dark line of the La Barra reef offshore, the white sail-shaped Alfredo Kraus auditorium at the far end, surfers at La Cicer
